notes: 200+ person consulting firm improving health and education outcomes in developing countries. Founded 2013 by ex-McKinsey public sector. Works with governments across 30+ countries. Innovative leader but consulting model, not tech company.

Philippine listed education holding company (PSE:IPO) under House of Investments / Yuchengco Group. Operates 7 institutions including Mapúa University, National Teachers College, APEC Schools. 60,000+ students. Merged with AC Education.

Education arm of Phinma Corporation. Network of 10 institutions serving 177,000+ students across Philippines and Indonesia. Focus on underserved youth, first-generation college students. Fast-growing.

Australian-founded nonprofit operating in East Africa (Uganda). Founded 2008. Education, healthcare, livelihood support. 4,500+ people reached. Annabelle has OAM. Locally-led, globally-supported model.
80 schools (56 primary, 24 secondary), 6 early learning centres, 43,000 students, 4,500 staff. Western Sydney, Australia. Diocese-level education system administration.

contacts.name: Stavros Yiannouka
contacts.title: CEO
contacts.relationship_context: Known contact. Web research suggests he may still be CEO (not former). Verify.

notes: Global education innovation platform. Part of Qatar Foundation, established 2009. Biennial summit, research programs, prizes. Think tank + convening platform, not an education provider. Stavros also sits on Blue Diagonal Capital board.

EdTech — student accommodation and events management software for higher education. Operates in 20+ countries, managing 700,000+ student bedrooms. Has tech team. Barry previously CEO of Double First, CRO at Education Horizons Group.

One of fastest-growing quality-focused education companies in Africa. Certified B Corp. Founded 2014 in Sierra Leone (Ebola response). 300,000 students, 700+ schools across Sierra Leone, Liberia, Ghana, Rwanda. Has tech team. Paul is ex-Tony Blair Africa Governance Initiative.
